summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #603 by adding Gemini 3.x models to the static catalog and updating capability detection (reasoning, chat filtering) for new model name patterns.
summary.outcome: Successfully updated the Gemini provider in lib.rs: added 3 Gemini 3.x models to the static catalog, extended reasoning support detection, added chat-capability filters for live/image Gemini model IDs, updated tests. All 261 provider tests pass, clippy and fmt clean.

repo: Dynamic model discovery via /v1beta/openai/models is already enabled for Gemini; static catalog serves as fallback only.
Context window (1M) and vision support for Gemini models are handled generically via starts_with("gemini-") so new Gemini versions don't need explicit entries for those capabilities.
The llama-cpp CMake build issue is a pre-existing problem tracked separately in moltis-csq; workspace-level clippy will always show it.
Beads is the issue tracker used for this project; workflow is: create issue, claim it, close it after commit.

The -image- contains-check for non-chat filtering only matches mid-string occurrences, safely distinguishing Gemini image model IDs from gpt-image/chatgpt-image prefixes which are caught by earlier prefix filters.

code.finding: GEMINI_MODELS static catalog needed Gemini 3 Flash, 3 Flash Lite, and 3.1 Pro added as fallback entries.
supports_reasoning_for_model() previously only matched gemini-2.5*; extended to also match gemini-3* patterns for thinking support.
is_chat_capable_model() needed new filters: contains("-live-") and contains("-image-") to exclude non-chat Gemini models returned by the discovery endpoint.

workflow: Run clippy scoped to a single crate (cargo clippy -p <crate>) to avoid noise from unrelated workspace build failures.
Always run cargo fmt after edits and verify with --check before committing to avoid CI failures.
Verify negative cases (e.g., that a new contains() filter won't accidentally match unrelated model IDs) before committing.

friction: Workspace-level clippy is noisy due to a pre-existing llama-cpp CMake build issue, requiring scoped clippy runs to get a clean signal.
A formatting issue was caught post-edit and required an extra fix-and-verify cycle before committing.
open_items: Gemini 3.x model IDs were inferred from Google API inspection; if Google changes naming conventions for future releases, the static catalog and capability detection patterns will need revisiting.
The llama-cpp CMake build issue (tracked in moltis-csq) remains unresolved and pollutes workspace-level tooling runs.
